events.title: Invesco QQQ Thanksgiving Day Half Marathon, 5K, Mile & Dash
The Polar Opposite Peachtree 2026
events.eventDescription: Register through the provided link. Ham radio operators will be coordinated by Atlanta ARES.

events.title: Winter Field Day
events.eventDescription: Winter Field Day is an annual amateur radio event held on the last full weekend in January that challenges operators to set up portable stations in cold, remote conditions to practice emergency communications.

events.title: Classic City Marathon
events.eventDescription: Athens Radio Club will be coordinating ham radio operators for this event. Bob Herrin - KE4JLL, the club Public Service coordinator can provide you with more details and updates.

events.title: Orlando Hamcation
events.eventDescription: Orlando HamCation is a three-day annual ham radio convention in Orlando, Florida that combines vendor exhibits, technical talks, awards and social events for amateur radio enthusiasts.

events.title: Publix Atlanta Marathon
events.eventDescription: Ham radio volunteers provide real-time health and welfare relays to public safety agencies along the March 1st course.
events.notes:
1. Go to the website
2. Enter password "PAMHAM2026" and click Submit
3. Register for the "Communications - HAM operator" slot

events.title: GA ARES Annual Training (ARES100)
events.eventDescription: Session covering the latest in emergency communications, incident response, and public service operations.
events.notes: All attendees MUST BE REGISTERED under GEMA rules. Registration is available at the meeting, but this process is much more time consuming than the check-in process for preregistered attendees.

events.title: Georgia Parks on the Air
events.eventDescription: Georgia Parks on the Air is a weekend-long contest that mobilizes ham operators to activate (or hunt from) every Georgia state park — about 50 parks — in a single 36-hour window, rewarding contacts made from those park sites.

events.title: Dayton Hamvention
events.eventDescription: Dayton Hamvention is one of the world's largest amateur radio conventions, held each May in the Dayton, Ohio area where thousands of hams gather for exhibits, vendors, workshops and community networking.

events.title: Northside Hospital Peachtree Road Race
events.eventDescription: The world's largest 10K road race. Ham radio operators provide communications support for the medical branch. Positions include Water Stops, pace cars, shadows, start and finish line assignments, and Call Takers in the Multi-Agency Coordination Center (MACC). You do not need to be an ARES member to participate. Call Taker positions do not require an Amateur Radio license.
events.notes:
1. Go to the sign-up website
2. Enter password "PRR26HAM" and click Submit
3. Select your preferred position (Water Stop, pace car, shadow, start/finish line, or MACC Call Taker)
4. If signing up as a Call Taker, enter "Call Taker" in the FCC Call Sign field — no Amateur Radio license required for this role

Sign up early so organizers can plan volunteer coverage for the medical branch.

events.title: Huntsville Hamfest
events.eventDescription: Huntsville Hamfest is a two-day ARRL-sanctioned gathering held each August at the Von Braun Center in Huntsville, Alabama, featuring a flea market, vendor displays and family-friendly activities for amateur radio enthusiasts.

events.title: No One Walks Alone
events.eventDescription: Pathways Center and AnotherWay Foundation, Inc. will host a community walk to honor loved ones that have lost their lives to suicide and to raise funds to support individuals seeking mental health treatment that are not able to afford care.

events.title: Ferst Lewis Grizzard and Catfish Bike Ride
events.eventDescription: Coweta Ferst Readers, Inc.'s mission is to provide a free monthly age-appropriate book for every child under the age of five in Coweta County.

events.title: Spin for Kids Group Bike Ride
PNC Atlanta 10 Mile and 5K
events.eventDescription: The Southeast's largest and most iconic 10 mile race. Scenic course, skyline views.

events.title: Snowflake 10k
events.eventDescription: The race is sponsored by the Winterville Civitan Club and supports the Firefly Trail, and persons who have disabilities.

events.title: Stone Mountain Hamfest
events.eventDescription: Annual amateur radio convention and flea market featuring vendors, exhibits, forums, and prizes for ham radio enthusiasts, electronics fans, and tech hobbyists.

events.title: Georgia QSO Party
events.eventDescription: Annual on-air amateur radio contest held the second full weekend in April, where participants contact stations in Georgia's 159 counties (and worldwide) on CW and SSB modes.

events.title: Georgia QSO Party
events.eventDescription: Annual on-air amateur radio contest held the second full weekend in April, where participants contact stations in Georgia's 159 counties (and worldwide) on CW and SSB modes.

events.title: FCARC POTA Party
events.eventDescription: Explore the POTA event, practice emergency/portable radio setups, and get hands-on experience. Bring your own equipment; if you don't have a radio, mine is available when not in use.

events.title: Winter POTA Event
events.eventLocation: Watson Mill Bridge State Park US-2207
events.eventDescription: Setup on Friday and operate for all weekend making POTA contacts.
events.notes: Visitors are invited to come out and play radio or just watch. The stations will be in the Equestrian Area Cabins on the East side of the park. The closest address is 1788 Watson Mill Bridge Road.

events.title: EXERCISE: Tornado Drill
events.eventDescription: On Feb 4th the Peachtree City NWS will conduct its yearly tornado drill. If inclement weather is happening that day, it will be either re-scheduled or possibly canceled. We will be linking in all the Georgia Skywarn Linked Repeaters at 8am and will start taking check-ins for the drill at 9am.
events.notes: Check-ins to the net will be taken by callsigns. Beginning with Alpha calls, then Whiskey calls, then November calls, then Kilo calls. This sequence will be repeated many times during the duration of the net. When checking in provide: Callsign, Name, County. Our biggest goal is to reach as many of the PTC NWS forecast area as possible. This is where relays may come in handy and is often wanted during severe weather outbreaks.

events.title: Winter Field Day
events.eventDescription: Winter Field Day is an annual amateur radio event held on the last full weekend in January that challenges operators to set up portable stations in cold, remote conditions to practice emergency communications.

events.title: Star Spangled Classic Fireworks
events.eventDescription: Athens Radio Club provides communications supporting parking and traffic management for Athens-Clarke County's annual Independence Day fireworks celebration.

events.title: Coach Mike's Run Through the Woods 5K
events.eventLocation: Athens YMCA, 915 Hawthorne Ave, Athens, GA 30605
events.eventDescription: Cross country 5K race in memory of Coach Mike Castronis. Athens Radio Club provides communications support. Course begins on the YMCA track with two loops through adjacent woods.

events.title: Colbert Trunk or Treat
events.eventLocation: Colbert City Park (Red Canna Park), Colbert, GA
events.eventDescription: Athens Radio Club provides communications and parking support for community Halloween event.

events.title: Invesco QQQ Thanksgiving Day Half Marathon, 5K, Mile & Dash
Peachy Trunkfest & Swap
events.eventDescription: A casual, vehicle-based tailgate swap meet hosted by the Fayette County Amateur Radio Club (KK4GQ). Buy, sell, and trade used amateur radio equipment — rigs, antennas, parts — directly from your trunk. FREE to attend and sell! Complimentary coffee and donuts while supplies last. Cash is highly recommended. Sellers should arrive 8:00am–8:45am. Talk-In: 145.210 (–) PL 131.8 Hz. Vehicle-based event only — no tents, external tables, amplified sound, alcohol, or open fires.

events.title: Dacula Memorial Day Parade
events.eventLocation: Hebron Baptist Church, 202 Hebron Church Rd, Dacula, GA 30019
events.eventDescription: Ham radio operators help coordinate the Dacula Memorial Day Parade by lining up participants (vehicles, floats, marching bands, groups, tractors, and horses) at Hebron Baptist Church so they depart in the correct order for announcement at the Announcing Stand.
events.notes: Arrive at 7:00 AM to help set up the pop-up tent, portable repeater, and tripod markers with flags. Wear comfortable shoes and clothing. Bring and wear your yellow safety vest and GARS Mission Hat (free hats available for new volunteers). Program your HT to the portable repeater: 147.045 MHz (minus offset), PL 82.5. No HT? No problem — you can shadow someone who has one. Chick-fil-A breakfast biscuits and coffee will be provided.

events.title: Chattahoochee Nature Center Possum Trot 10K
events.eventLocation: Chattahoochee Nature Center, 9135 Willeo Road, Roswell, GA
events.eventDescription: The 48th Annual Possum Trot 10K is a Peachtree Road Race qualifier benefiting the Chattahoochee Nature Center's environmental education and wildlife rehabilitation programs. Last year drew more than 800 runners. Ham radio operators provide the only effective means of communicating field conditions back to race organizers and emergency personnel — stationed at the starting line, mile markers, turnarounds, and course waypoints. Operators watch for and report injured or in-distress runners, vehicles on the closed course, and other safety issues, and coordinate the start of mile-marker race timers.
events.notes:
Race starts 7:00 AM. Roads and parking lot close at 6:00 AM — volunteers should arrive at CNC no later than 5:30 AM. Roads reopen 90 minutes after race start; finished by ~10:00 AM. Bring a 2m HT with a good battery (and a spare if you have one). Volunteers receive a limited-edition Possum Trot T-shirt.

To volunteer, reply to the Groups.io thread (linked above) or contact Mike (K4ZEC) or Janet (KN4JNC) Carroll directly.
